record.field: PostHog is an open-source product analytics platform with event tracking, funnels, session replay, feature flags, experiments and surveys.

PostHog is an open-source product analytics platform with event tracking, funnels, session replay, feature flags, experiments and surveys.

It bundles the tools a software team needs to understand how people use a product into a single self-hostable suite, positioning itself as an all-in-one alternative to stitching together Amplitude, Mixpanel, LaunchDarkly, Hotjar and Optimizely. The stack is built on Django, ClickHouse for event storage, PostgreSQL for application data, Redis for queues and Kafka for ingestion, so every event and recording stays on your own infrastructure. It targets engineering, product and growth teams that want to own their user data and run analysis without shipping it to third parties.

Key features
  • Product analytics with events, trends, funnels, retention and user paths
  • Session replay to watch real user interactions and debug UX issues
  • Feature flags and staged rollouts, plus A/B tests and experiments
  • Surveys, cohorts, group analytics and an interactive SQL query editor
  • Autocapture and SDKs for web, mobile and server, plus a REST API and webhooks
  • Fully self-hosted: PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Redis and Kafka keep data in-house
Best for

Engineering, product and growth teams that need a complete, privacy-owned product-analytics suite and are comfortable operating a heavy multi-service stack. It is resource intensive (several GB of RAM and ample disk); for simple cookie-free web stats a lighter tool like Plausible or Matomo is a better fit. For large production deployments PostHog's official installer is the recommended path.

services: db: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER} - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 12 volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' expose: - '5432' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:6.2.7-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: redis-server --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru --maxmemory 200mb healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 12 volumes: - ./redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' expose: - '6379' networks: - internal zookeeper: image: zookeeper:3.7.1 restart: unless-stopped environment: - ZOO_AUTOPURGE_INTERVAL=1 volumes: - ./zookeeper_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_zookeeper' expose: - '2181' networks: - internal kafka: image: bitnami/kafka:2.8.1 restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - zookeeper environment: - KAFKA_BROKER_ID=1001 - KAFKA_CFG_RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID=1001 - KAFKA_CFG_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092 - KAFKA_CFG_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092 - KAFKA_CFG_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT=zookeeper:2181 - ALLOW_PLAINTEXT_LISTENER=yes - KAFKA_CFG_MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES=52428800 volumes: - ./kafka_data:/bitnami/kafka container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_kafka' expose: - '9092' networks: - internal clickhouse: image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3.3.102-alpine restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - zookeeper environment: - CLICKHOUSE_DB=posthog volumes: - ./clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse - ./config/clickhouse-config.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/default.xml:ro - ./config/clickhouse-users.xml:/etc/clickhouse-server/users.d/default.xml:ro ulimits: nofile: soft: 262144 hard: 262144 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_clickhouse' expose: - '8123' - '9000' networks: - internal object_storage: image: minio/minio:latest restart: unless-stopped command: server --address :19000 --console-address :19001 /data environment: - MINIO_ROOT_USER=${OBJECT_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID} - MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=${OBJECT_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY} volumes: - ./object_storage:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_object_storage' expose: - '19000' - '19001' networks: - internal web: image: posthog/posthog:latest restart: unless-stopped command: ./bin/docker-server depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy clickhouse: condition: service_started kafka: condition: service_started object_storage: condition: service_started environment: &app_env - SECRET_KEY=${POSTHOG_SECRET} - ENCRYPTION_SALT_KEYS=${ENCRYPTION_SALT_KEYS} - SITE_URL=https://{{ DOMAIN }} - DATABASE_URL=postgres://${POSTGRES_USER}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/${POSTGRES_DB} - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/ - CLICKHOUSE_HOST=clickhouse - CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE=posthog - CLICKHOUSE_USER=default - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD= - CLICKHOUSE_SECURE=false - CLICKHOUSE_VERIFY=false - KAFKA_HOSTS=kafka:9092 - KAFKA_URL=kafka://kafka:9092 - OBJECT_STORAGE_ENABLED=true - OBJECT_STORAGE_ENDPOINT=http://object_storage:19000 - OBJECT_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${OBJECT_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID} - OBJECT_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${OBJECT_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY} - OBJECT_STORAGE_BUCKET=posthog - DISABLE_SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=true - IS_BEHIND_PROXY=true - TRUST_ALL_PROXIES=true - DEBUG=0 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network worker: image: posthog/posthog:latest restart: unless-stopped command: ./bin/docker-worker-celery --with-scheduler depends_on: - db - redis - clickhouse - kafka - object_storage environment: *app_env container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_worker' networks: - internal plugins: image: posthog/posthog:latest restart: unless-stopped command: ./bin/plugin-server depends_on: - db - redis - clickhouse - kafka - object_storage environment: *app_env container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_plugins' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (datos de aplicación) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # ClickHouse/object_storage/kafka se respaldan por tar de sus volúmenes (no excluidos). docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
Django SECRET_KEY used to sign sessions, cookies and tokens.
Fernet key used to encrypt sensitive fields at rest (integrations, credentials).
Password for the internal PostgreSQL application database.
Secret key for the internal MinIO object storage (session recordings, exports).
User for the internal PostgreSQL application database.
Name of the internal PostgreSQL application database.
Access key ID for the internal MinIO object storage.

<clickhouse> <logger> <level>warning</level> <console>true</console> </logger> <listen_host>0.0.0.0</listen_host> <!-- Coordinación para tablas ReplicatedMergeTree que crea PostHog --> <zookeeper> <node> <host>zookeeper</host> <port>2181</port> </node> </zookeeper> <macros> <shard>01</shard> <replica>replica-01</replica> </macros> <!-- Reducir logging interno para ahorrar disco --> <query_thread_log remove="remove"/> <query_log remove="remove"/> <text_log remove="remove"/> <trace_log remove="remove"/> <metric_log remove="remove"/> <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/> <session_log remove="remove"/> <part_log remove="remove"/> </clickhouse>
